ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the sensor is installed */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the sensor */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was installed */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was last calibrated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the sensor (e.g., active, inactive, maintenance) */,
  "sensor_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of sensor */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Unique serial number for each sensor */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the sensor */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Last date the sensor was maintained */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Next scheduled maintenance date */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments about the sensor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensors_sensor_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_type_id") REFERENCES sensor_types ("sensor_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_types" (
  "sensor_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sensor type */,
  "measurement_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency at which the sensor type measures data (e.g., every second, every minute) */,
  "data_retention_period" INTEGER /* How long data from this sensor type should be retained */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that collected the data */,
  "time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the data point (in UTC) */,
  "local_time" REAL /* Local timestamp of the data point */,
  "stream_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data stream to which this data point belongs */,
  "sensor_parameter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the specific parameter measured */,
  "value" REAL /* Value measured by the sensor */,
  "units" TEXT /* Units of the measured value */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_data_sensor_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_parameter_id") REFERENCES sensor_parameters ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_data_stream_id FOREIGN KEY ("stream_id") REFERENCES data_streams ("stream_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_data_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_parameters" (
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parameter */,
  "sensor_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor type that measures this parameter */,
  "parameter_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the parameter */,
  "min_value" REAL /* Minimum valid value for the parameter */,
  "max_value" REAL /* Maximum valid value for the parameter */,
  "threshold_low" REAL /* Low threshold value for anomaly detection */,
  "threshold_high" REAL /* High threshold value for anomaly detection */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_parameters_sensor_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_type_id") REFERENCES sensor_types ("sensor_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_streams" (
  "stream_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data stream */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that generated the stream */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parameter measured in the stream */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the data stream */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the data stream */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the data stream (e.g., active, paused, completed) */,
  "frequency" TEXT /* Frequency at which data is collected in this stream */,
  "last_update_time" TEXT /* Last time the stream was updated */,
  "data_retention_period" INTEGER /* How long data in this stream should be retained */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stream_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_streams_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_streams_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES sensor_parameters ("parameter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_anomalies" (
  "anomaly_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each anomaly */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point where the anomaly was detected */,
  "anomaly_type" TEXT /* Type of anomaly (e.g., high value, low value, sudden change) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the anomaly */,
  "detected_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the anomaly was detected */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the anomaly (e.g., new, acknowledged, resolved) */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the anomaly (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "assigned_to" INTEGER /* ID of the user to whom the anomaly is assigned */,
  "resolution_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the anomaly was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("anomaly_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_anomalies_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES sensor_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_anomalies_assigned_to FOREIGN KEY ("assigned_to") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_records" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance record */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that received maintenance */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance (e.g., calibration, repair, replacement) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance activity */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the maintenance */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the maintenance */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the maintenance (e.g., scheduled, in progress, completed) */,
  "assigned_to" INTEGER /* ID of the user responsible for the maintenance */,
  "resolution_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the maintenance was completed */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Next scheduled maintenance date */,
  "cost" REAL /* Cost of the maintenance activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_assigned_to FOREIGN KEY ("assigned_to") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, data analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login_time" TEXT /* Last time the user logged in */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point or maintenance record being accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit, download) */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the access occurred */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "device" TEXT /* Device used to access the system (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES sensor_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., high value, low value, anomaly detected) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "triggered_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was triggered */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the alert (e.g., new, acknowledged, resolved) */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "assigned_to" INTEGER /* ID of the user to whom the alert is assigned */,
  "resolution_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_alerts_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_alerts_assigned_to FOREIGN KEY ("assigned_to") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_maintenance_schedules" (
  "schedule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance schedule */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor for which the maintenance is scheduled */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance (e.g., calibration, repair, replacement) */,
  "scheduled_start_time" TEXT /* Scheduled start time of the maintenance */,
  "scheduled_end_time" TEXT /* Scheduled end time of the maintenance */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the maintenance schedule (e.g., scheduled, in progress, completed) */,
  "assigned_to" INTEGER /* ID of the user responsible for the scheduled maintenance */,
  "estimated_cost" REAL /* Estimated cost of the maintenance activity */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments about the schedule */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("schedule_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_maintenance_schedules_assigned_to FOREIGN KEY ("assigned_to") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_maintenance_schedules_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor for which the version is recorded */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the sensor data */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date when the version was created */,
  "changes" TEXT /* Description of changes made in this version */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the version (e.g., active, archived) */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the version */,
  "approved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who approved the version */,
  "approval_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the version was approved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_versions_approved_by FOREIGN KEY ("approved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_versions_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_versions_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)
